GlideScript
untitled.gs
Editor
Getting Started Pages & Navigation Text & Spacing Components Variables & State Styling System Full Examples

GlideScript Language Reference

GlideScript is a declarative, design-focused programming language designed to build single-page web applications. By focusing on fluid viewport layouts, layout scaling, and state-driven bindings, GlideScript lets you write lightweight code while the compiler generates a fully optimized standalone HTML/CSS/JS application.

How it works: The editor compiles your GlideScript code on the fly and renders it inside the Live Preview pane. You can export the output as a single, self-contained HTML file ready for Vercel, Netlify, or local hosting.

Pages & Navigation

Every GlideScript application is composed of one or more pages. A page block is declared using the `pg.[page_name] [ ... ]` syntax.

  • Default Page: The first page declared in your source code is treated as the initial home page.
  • Auto Navigation: If your script contains 2 or more visible pages, the compiler generates a sleek top navigation bar automatically.
  • Hidden Pages: You can hide a page from the navigation bar (e.g. for sub-pages or administration panels) by including the `pg.hide = true` directive inside the page block.
// Define the home page (visible by default)
pg.home [
  text = "Welcome to the Home Page"
  add.button(text = "Go to Admin (Hidden)", click = open pg.admin, colour = #2a2a38, edges = rounded)
]

// Define a hidden page (accessible only via navigation triggers)
pg.admin [
  pg.hide = true
  text = "Welcome to the Secret Admin Panel!"
  add.button(text = "Return Home", click = open pg.home, edges = rounded)
]

Text & Spacing

Standard layout flows rely on fluid typography and vertical height spacing.

  • Text Elements: Defined using `text = "your text content"`. This compiles to standard, fluid paragraphs using viewport font sizing. The content is automatically escaped to prevent XSS attacks.
  • Spacing Elements: Defined using `space.N` where `N` is an integer representing height in viewport height (`vh`) units. For example, `space.10` creates a vertical spacer taking up 10% of the screen height.
pg.home [
  text = "This is a line of text at the top."
  space.5 // 5vh vertical gap
  text = "This is a line of text below the gap."
  space.20 // 20vh large vertical spacer
  text = "Footer text at the bottom."
]

Components

Components are added inside a page block using the `add.[type](args)` syntax. GlideScript is tolerant of typos, automatically mapping `add.botton` to standard button elements.

1. Box Component (`add.box`)

Renders a container card used to highlight information, group items, or create backdrops.

Argument Type Description
text String Text content rendered in the center of the box.
colour / color Hex / CSS Color Background color of the box card.
size W and H Width in vw and height in vh. E.g., 60 and 25.

2. Button Component (`add.button` / `add.botton`)

Renders an interactive clickable button element.

Argument Type Description
text String Text label printed inside the button.
click Action Navigation event. Format: open pg.[page_name].

3. Textbox Component (`add.textbox`)

Renders a responsive input text box capturing state.

Argument Type Description
placeholder String Instructional text shown inside the input when empty.
var Variable Name Reactive state variable bound to the value of this textbox.

4. Image Component (`add.image`)

Renders an image from a URL or relative path.

Argument Type Description
src / source URL / Path The location of the image file.
alt String Accessibility fallback description for the image. 
pg.home [
  text = "Exploring Elements"
  space.2

  // A rounded decorative card
  add.box(text = "Information Card", colour = #e8f4f8, size = 80 and 15, edges = rounded)
  space.2

  // An image element
  add.image(src = "https://picsum.photos/400/200", alt = "Random Image", size = 80 and 25, edges = rounded)
  space.2

  // A button linking to the about page
  add.button(text = "Read More About Us", click = open pg.about, colour = #6e6eff, edges = rounded)
]

pg.about [
  text = "About Us"
  add.button(text = "Go Back", click = open pg.home, edges = rounded)
]

Variables & Reactive State

GlideScript supports real-time text binding based on user input. You can bind input text boxes to specific variables, which immediately update downstream layout nodes without reloading the page.

  • Register Variable: Set `var = [variable_name]` inside `add.textbox(...)`.
  • Bind Variable: Place the `when var.[variable_name] has value` directive immediately *before* a text block, box, or button.
pg.home [
  text = "State Bindings Demo"
  space.2

  text = "Type your nickname below:"
  add.textbox(placeholder = "Nickname...", var = user_nick, colour = #ffffff, size = 60 and 6, edges = rounded)

  space.4

  // This text block will automatically bind to whatever is typed above
  when var.user_nick has value
  text = "Hello, guest!" // Default text is shown before user types
]

Styling System

All components (`box`, `button`, `textbox`, `image`) support custom properties inside their parameter parenthesis. These allow precise layout and skinning controls:

Property Syntax Example Effect
colour / color colour = #2d6a4f Sets the background color of the element.
text-colour / textcolor textcolor = #ffffff Sets the typography color inside the element.
size size = 50 and 15 Sets dimensions: Width of 50vw and Height of 15vh.
location location = 10 and 20 Positively indexes element: absolute position of left 10vw, top 20vh.
edges edges = rounded Applies a 12px border radius to smooth corners.
font-size / fontsize fontsize = 2.5 Sets font size to 2.5vh (scales on screen size).
opacity opacity = 0.8 Sets opacity (0.0 fully hidden to 1.0 solid).
border border = "2px solid red" Applies custom CSS borders. Use string quotes.

Full Application Examples

Here are some cohesive, complete scripts demonstrating the versatility of GlideScript. Load them directly to test!

Example A: The Client Portal

pg.login [
  text = "Client Portal"
  space.4

  text = "Enter your Client Access Key:"
  add.textbox(placeholder = "Client Key...", var = key, colour = #ffffff, size = 60 and 6, edges = rounded)

  space.2
  add.button(text = "Go to Portal", click = open pg.portal, colour = #1f1f2e, edges = rounded)
]

pg.portal [
  text = "Security Portal Status"
  space.2

  when var.key has value
  add.box(text = "Loading workspace...", colour = #d4edda, size = 80 and 12, edges = rounded)

  space.4
  text = "Available workspaces:"
  add.button(text = "General Database", click = open pg.db, colour = #2d6a4f, edges = rounded)
  add.button(text = "Lock & Logout", click = open pg.login, colour = #6c757d, edges = rounded)
]

pg.db [
  pg.hide = true
  text = "Database Mainframe"
  space.2
  add.box(text = "Connecting to remote database instances... Success.", colour = #e8f4f8, size = 80 and 20, edges = rounded)
  space.4
  add.button(text = "Return to Portal", click = open pg.portal, edges = rounded)
]

Example B: Compact Profile

pg.home [
  space.5
  add.image(src = "https://picsum.photos/150/150", alt = "User avatar", size = 20 and 20, edges = rounded, location = 40 and 10)
  
  space.25
  text = "Sarah Jenkins"
  text = "Lead UI Designer & Creative Technologist"
  
  space.4
  add.box(text = "Hello! I design lightweight interfaces and custom application designs using declarative schemas like GlideScript.", colour = #fcf8e3, size = 80 and 15, edges = rounded)
  
  space.4
  add.button(text = "View Portfolio", click = open pg.portfolio, colour = #e84545, edges = rounded)
]

pg.portfolio [
  text = "Sarah's Creative Portfolio"
  space.2
  add.box(text = "Project A: Fluid Layout Engine", colour = #e8f4f8, size = 85 and 10, edges = rounded)
  space.2
  add.box(text = "Project B: SVG Interface Builder", colour = #fff3cd, size = 85 and 10, edges = rounded)
  space.4
  add.button(text = "Return to Profile", click = open pg.home, edges = rounded)
]
📄
Drop your .gs file
Release to load it into the editor
